response: The task is to calculate the total number of power punches landed by a heavyweight boxer across three matches. The first match saw 120 power punches. The second match increased the number of power punches by 25%, which means the number of punches in the second match is 120 + (25% of 120). The third match had 30 more power punches than the second match. The total number of power punches across all three matches is the sum of the punches from each match. The first match had 120 punches. The second match had 120 + (25% of 120) = 120 + 30 = 150 punches. The third match had 150 + 30 = 180 punches. The total number of power punches is 120 + 150 + 180 = 450 punches.
